title: Gap 3: Simple roots of T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) not verified in reduction

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Description

The reduction argument requires T_eps p, T_eps q, AND (T_eps p) boxplus_n (T_eps q) = T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) to all have simple roots so that the simple-root proof applies. The solution verifies simple roots for T_eps p and T_eps q via Nuij's theorem, but never verifies that T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) has simple roots.

## Mathematical Detail

Nuij's theorem (Math. Scand. 23, 1968): if r is a monic real-rooted polynomial of degree n and eps > 0, then (1 - eps d/dx)^n r has simple roots.

Here T_eps^2 = (1 - eps d/dx)^{2n}. Applied to p boxplus_n q (degree n, real-rooted), we can factor:
  T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) = (1 - eps d/dx)^n [(1 - eps d/dx)^n (p boxplus_n q)]

Step 1: (1 - eps d/dx)^n (p boxplus_n q) is real-rooted with simple roots by Nuij (applied to the degree-n real-rooted polynomial p boxplus_n q).
Step 2: (1 - eps d/dx)^n applied to a degree-n polynomial with simple real roots again gives a degree-n polynomial with simple real roots by Nuij.

Therefore T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) has simple roots. This two-step argument is absent from the solution.

## What needs to be proved

Apply Nuij's theorem twice to establish that T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) has simple roots for every eps > 0.
